nodes.description: Long photoperiod input integrating clock phase with light duration; drives CO accumulation in leaves.
Blue light activation of FKF1 (and cryptochromes) for photoperiod sensing.
Low-temperature signal activating CBFs and VIN3 initiation.
Extended cold triggering epigenetic silencing of FLC; competence to flower acquired after weeks of cold.
Above-optimal temperature activating PIF4, destabilising SVP, and directly promoting FT.
Scaffolding factor recruiting the FRI complex to FLC chromatin.
CLF/SWN-VRN2/FIE/MSI1 complex depositing H3K27me3 at FLC.
F-box protein of SCF^SLY1; Perception-Gate co-receptor for DELLA ubiquitination.
Rate-limiting GA biosynthesis enzyme.
GA-inactivating enzyme (Motif 4 Biosynthesis-Degradation Balance).
Autonomous-pathway RNA-binding protein; represses FLC and SVP.
Autonomous-pathway RRM protein repressing FLC.
LSD1-family H3K4 demethylase at FLC.
WD40 component of HDAC complex; represses FLC and SVP.
Homeodomain-containing autonomous repressor of FLC.
Clock-regulated scaffold; activates FKF1/CO/FT, represses CDF1 and PIF4. Constitutive in this simplification.
Photosynthate whose rising levels with age suppress miR156 and feed T6P-mediated florigen induction.
Catalytic enzyme producing T6P from sucrose; also has a direct FT-promoter role.
Juvenile-phase miRNA silencing SPL transcripts.
Sugar-status signalling metabolite activating FT in the shoot apex.
Blue-light-activated F-box; degrades CDFs, stabilises CO, activates FT.
Composite for CDF1/2/3/5; morning DOF repressors of CO and direct FT-promoter binders.
Photoperiod output; activates FT in leaves.
Cold-induced PHD-finger recruiting PRC2 to FLC.
MADS-box central floral repressor; represses FT and SOC1.
MADS-box repressor; forms SVP-FLC complex; mutually represses SOC1 (Motif 7).
bHLH activator of FT under warm temperatures; inhibited by DELLA and GI.
Bioactive GAs; ligand for GID1. Balanced by GA20OX/GA2OX (Motif 4).
GA receptor; co-inhibitor of DELLA with SLY1 (Perception Gate).
Composite repressor; Multi-Output Scaffold (Motif 5) targeting SPL9/LFY/SOC1/CO/PIF4.
Adult-phase TF activating miR172 and integrators (AP1/LFY/SOC1/FUL).
Adult-phase miRNA cleaving AP2-clade transcripts; Motif 7 mutual inhibition with AP2.
AP2-like repressor clade; mutually inhibited with SOC1 and miR172 (Motif 7 x2).
Mobile florigen; activated by CO/FKF1/GI/PIF4/T6P/TPS1/Warm; repressed by FLC/SVP/CDF1.
MADS-box floral-integrator redundant with SOC1/AP1; activated by FT/SPL9/SOC1; repressed by TFL1.
Meristem bZIP; partners with FT (activator) and TFL1 (repressor complex).
MADS-box floral integrator; mutually represses SVP and AP2 (Motif 7 x2).
MADS-box integrator reinforcing LFY; vernalization-inducible.
Floral-meristem identity MADS-box; AP1<->TFL1 mutual inhibition (Motif 7).
Plant-specific TF specifying floral-meristem identity.
FT paralog maintaining inflorescence indeterminacy; Multi-Output Scaffold repressing AP1/LFY/SOC1/FUL/Flowering_Time, partnering with FD.
Time to flowering; increased = earlier, decreased = later.

edges.mechanism: LD afternoon coincides with CO mRNA peak (driven by FKF1-GI-mediated CDF degradation) and stabilizes CO protein; SD degrades CO

evidence.title: Circadian Clock and Photoperiodic Flowering in Arabidopsis: CONSTANS Is a Hub for Signal Integration
evidence.authors: Shim JS, Kubota A, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CO protein is stabilized in LD conditions but degraded in SD conditions

edges.mechanism: Blue light activates FKF1 LOV domain to form FKF1-GI complex

evidence.title: FKF1 and GIGANTEA complex formation is required for day-length measurement in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sawa M, Nusinow DA, Kay SA, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: FKF1 and GI proteins form a complex in a blue-light dependent manner

edges.mechanism: GI physically binds FKF1 in a blue-light-dependent manner via FKF1 LOV domain; stabilizes FKF1 nuclear abundance

evidence.title: FKF1 and GIGANTEA complex formation is required for day-length measurement in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sawa M, Nusinow DA, Kay SA, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: FKF1 and GI proteins form a complex in a blue-light dependent manner
evidence.claim: GI and FKF1 form a blue-light-dependent complex that stabilizes FKF1

edges.mechanism: FKF1 forms SCF(FKF1) E3 ligase complex with ASK1, ASK2, CUL1 and targets CDF1 for ubiquitin-mediated degradation in late afternoon

evidence.title: FKF1 and GIGANTEA complex formation is required for day-length measurement in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sawa M, Nusinow DA, Kay SA, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: FKF1 regulates CO by degrading its repressor, CDF1

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CDF repression of CO is released in the afternoon through the action of a blue light induced complex composed of the GIGANTEA (GI) protein and the LOV domain blue-light photoreceptor E3 ubiquitin ligase FLAVIN BINDING, KELCH REPEAT, F-BOX 1 (FKF1)

edges.mechanism: GI forms complex with FKF1 that degrades CDFs in afternoon

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CDF repression of CO is released in the afternoon through the action of a blue light induced complex composed of the GIGANTEA (GI) protein and the LOV domain blue-light photoreceptor E3 ubiquitin ligase FLAVIN BINDING, KELCH REPEAT, F-BOX 1 (FKF1)

edges.mechanism: CDF1-5 bind DOF cis-elements in CO promoter in the morning and recruit TOPLESS/TPR co-repressors

evidence.title: Photoperiodic flowering in Arabidopsis: Multilayered regulatory mechanisms of CONSTANS and the florigen FLOWERING LOCUS T
evidence.authors: Takagi H, Hempton AK, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CYCLING DOF FACTOR 1 (CDF1) to CDF5 were the first CO regulators identified, strongly repressing CO transcription by recruiting TOPLESS(TPL)/TPL-RELATED (TPR) co-repressors

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: Repression of CO expression during the morning...is mediated by the CYCLING DOF FACTOR (CDF) family of DOF domain transcription factors

edges.mechanism: GI activates CO expression via FKF1-mediated degradation of CDF repressors; GI also partners with FKF1 to stabilize afternoon CO protein

evidence.title: The GI-CDF module of Arabidopsis affects freezing tolerance and growth as well as flowering
evidence.authors: Fornara F, de Montaigu A, Sanchez-Villarreal A, Zafra A, Davis SJ, Alfaro J, Davis AM, Schneeberger K, Coupland G
evidence.evidence_sentence: GI was shown to activate expression of the key floral regulators CONSTANS (CO) and FLOWERING LOCUS T (FT) by facilitating degradation of a family of CYCLING DOF FACTOR (CDF) transcriptional repressors
evidence.claim: GI activates CO by facilitating CDF degradation

edges.mechanism: FKF1 binds CO through LOV domain in blue-light-enhanced manner; prevents COP1 homodimer formation, stabilizing CO in LD afternoon

evidence.title: Distinct roles of FKF1, GIGANTEA, and ZEITLUPE proteins in the regulation of CONSTANS stability in Arabidopsis photoperiodic flowering
evidence.authors: Song YH, Estrada DA, Johnson RS, Li S, Kim SY, Kiba T, Kay SA, MacCoss MJ, Imaizumi T
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
evidence.evidence_sentence: FKF1 binds to CO through the LOV domain in a blue light-enhanced manner and stabilizes CO specifically in the LD afternoon
evidence.claim: FKF1 stabilizes CO protein in LD afternoon

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: Under blue light conditions, FKF1, in addition to its described role in CDF degradation, also stabilizes CO protein

edges.mechanism: CO binds directly to CORE cis-elements in FT promoter; CO-NF-Y complex brings distal NF-Y binding sites to proximal CORE via chromatin looping

evidence.title: Circadian Clock and Photoperiodic Flowering in Arabidopsis: CONSTANS Is a Hub for Signal Integration
evidence.authors: Shim JS, Kubota A, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: CO binds to the cis-element referred to as CO Responsive Element (CORE) in the FT promoter

evidence.title: Beyond the Genetic Pathways, Flowering Regulation Complexity in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Quiroz S, Yustis JC, Chavez-Hernandez EC, Martinez T, Sanchez MP, Garay-Arroyo A, Alvarez-Buylla ER, Garcia-Ponce B
evidence.evidence_sentence: CO â†’ FT: Binding to CO-responsive elements (CORE)

evidence.title: Photoperiodic Regulation of Florigen Function in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Golembeski GS, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: Primary activation of FT in the late afternoon of long day conditions is accomplished through the action of CO protein

edges.mechanism: FRI forms scaffold complex (FRI-C) with FRL1/FES1/SUF4/FLX to activate FLC via chromatin modifier recruitment

evidence.title: Genetic and Epigenetic Mechanisms Underlying Vernalization
evidence.authors: Kim DH, Sung S
evidence.evidence_sentence: FRI mainly acts to up-regulate FLC transcription and thus contributes to the vernalization requirement

edges.mechanism: Prolonged cold progressively induces VIN3 transcription

evidence.title: Mechanisms underlying vernalization-mediated VIN3 induction
evidence.authors: Kim DH, Zografos BR, Sung S
evidence.evidence_sentence: The most direct link between measurement of the duration of cold and the output of vernalization response is the induction of VIN3 expression, which is tightly linked to prolonged cold exposure

edges.mechanism: Vernalizing cold progressively activates VIN3 via H3K4me3

evidence.title: Polycomb-like complex
evidence.authors: Wood CC et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: VIN3 mRNA is present at very low abundance during growth at warm temperatures, with expression increasing progressively during vernalization

edges.mechanism: VIN3 PHD protein binds H3K9me2 and recruits PRC2 for H3K27me3 silencing

evidence.title: Genetic and Epigenetic Mechanisms Underlying Vernalization
evidence.authors: Kim DH, Sung S
evidence.evidence_sentence: all VIN3 family proteins preferentially bind to H3K9me2 peptides in vitro through PHD motifs
evidence.claim: VIN3 recruits PRC2 to silence FLC

edges.mechanism: PRC2 catalyzes H3K27me3 at FLC

evidence.title: Transcriptome and epigenome analyses of vernalization
evidence.authors: Xi Y et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: PRC2 catalyzes tri-methylation of histone H3K27

edges.mechanism: FCA autonomous-pathway RBP represses FLC

evidence.title: FRI-Independent Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Werner JD et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLC is also negatively regulated by the autonomous pathway

evidence.title: Autonomous Pathway: FLOWERING LOCUS C Repression through an Antisense-Mediated Chromatin-Silencing Mechanism
evidence.authors: Wu Z, Fang X, Zhu D, Dean C
evidence.evidence_sentence: FCA is a nuclear RNA-binding protein that functions in regulating alternative RNA 3'-end processing. FCA, FPA, and FY activities promote proximal polyadenylation of COOLAIR.
evidence.claim: FCA represses FLC via COOLAIR processing

edges.mechanism: FPA autonomous-pathway RBP represses FLC

evidence.title: FRI-Independent
evidence.authors: Werner JD et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLC is also negatively regulated by the autonomous pathway

evidence.title: FPA, a Gene Involved in Floral Induction in Arabidopsis, Encodes a Protein Containing RNA-Recognition Motifs
evidence.authors: Schomburg FM, Patton DA, Meinke DW, Amasino RM
evidence.evidence_sentence: The predicted FPA protein contains three RNA recognition motifs in the N-terminal region; autonomous-pathway genes act to decrease FLC.

edges.mechanism: FLD autonomous-pathway H3K4 demethylase represses FLC

evidence.title: Genetic and Epigenetic Mechanisms
evidence.authors: Kim DH, Sung S
evidence.evidence_sentence: Mutations in autonomous pathway genes result in high level of FLC expression

evidence.title: Autonomous Pathway: FLOWERING LOCUS C Repression through an Antisense-Mediated Chromatin-Silencing Mechanism
evidence.authors: Wu Z, Fang X, Zhu D, Dean C
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLD is a histone Lys-4 demethylase; FLD demethylates H3K4me2 mainly in the body of the FLC gene.
evidence.claim: FLD represses FLC by H3K4me2 demethylation

edges.mechanism: FVE recruits HDA6 to FLC

evidence.title: HOS1
evidence.authors: Jung JH, Park CM
evidence.evidence_sentence: HOS1 physically interacts with FVE, and FVE is essential for the binding of HOS1 to FLC chromatin

evidence.title: FVE, an Arabidopsis homologue of the retinoblastoma-associated protein that regulates flowering time and cold response, binds to chromatin as a large multiprotein complex
evidence.authors: Jeon J, Kim J
evidence.evidence_sentence: In the fve mutants, the FLC chromatin was enriched in acetylated histones H3 and H4, thereby indicating that FVE is required for histone deacetylation at the FLC chromatin.
evidence.claim: FVE represses FLC via histone deacetylation

edges.mechanism: LD is a homeodomain-containing transcriptional repressor of FLC; interacts with FLD and SDG26 in autonomous pathway complex

evidence.title: Characterization of an autonomous pathway complex that promotes flowering in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Qi PL, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: LD is a homeodomain-containing protein that functions as a transcriptional repressor of FLC; SDG26 interacts with the autonomous pathway components LD and FLD and thereby forms a complex that promotes flowering by suppressing FLC expression.

edges.mechanism: FLC-SVP heterodimer binds first intron of FT and represses transcription

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP protein is rapidly degraded by the 26S proteasome pathway in response to warm ambient temperatures

edges.mechanism: Warm temperature releases ELF3/HY5 repression and stabilizes PIF4 via reduced phyB activity

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Warm temperatures reduce the binding of the evening complex to chromatin and eventually promotes the expression of PIF4

evidence.authors: Takagi H, Hempton AK, Imaizumi T

evidence.title: Ambient Temperature-Responsive Mechanisms Coordinate Regulation of Flowering Time
evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 plays an important role in the activation of FT expression at warm ambient temperature
evidence.claim: PIF4 directly activates FT at warm temperature

edges.mechanism: GA20ox catalyzes the penultimate step in bioactive GA production

evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of GA20ox: early flowering and increased stem growth phenotypes

edges.mechanism: Bioactive GA binds the GID1 receptor, enabling GID1 to interact with DELLA proteins

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: GID1s bind to bioactive GAs with high affinity, but not to inactive GA derivatives

edges.mechanism: GID1-GA-DELLA complex recruits SCF-SLY1 E3 ligase that polyubiquitinates DELLA for 26S proteasome degradation

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA de-represses its signaling pathway by binding to GID1s, which induce degradation of GA signaling repressors (DELLAs) via an ubiquitin-proteasome pathway
evidence.claim: GID1 promotes DELLA degradation upon GA binding

edges.mechanism: SLY1 F-box protein recognizes GA-GID1-bound DELLA and triggers its polyubiquitination/degradation

evidence.title: Lifting DELLA Repression of Arabidopsis Seed Germination by Nonproteolytic Gibberellin Signaling
evidence.authors: Ariizumi T, Hauvermale AL, Nelson SK, Hanada A, Yamaguchi S, Steber CM
evidence.evidence_sentence: GID1-GA-DELLA complex formation stimulates DELLA recognition by the F-box protein SLY1, leading to DELLA polyubiquitination

edges.mechanism: DELLA proteins (RGA, GAI) directly bind SPL9 and interfere with SPL9 transcriptional activity

evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA directly binds to microRNA156 (miR156)-targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE (SPL) transcription factors; the interaction between DELLA and SPL interferes with SPL transcriptional activity.

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors

evidence.evidence_sentence: GA-deficient ga1-3 mutants fail to flower when grown under short days, with inability to upregulate LFY

evidence.authors: Susila H, Nasim Z, Ahn JH

evidence.authors: Zheng C, Ye M, Sang M, Wu R
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sugar promotes vegetative phase change in Arabidopsis thaliana by repressing the expression of MIR156A and MIR156C.

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, Horrer D, Zhang TQ, Hao YH, Feng YQ, Wang S, Schmid M, Wang JW

evidence.authors: Wu G, Park MY, Conway SR, Wang JW, Weigel D, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL9 is a direct transcriptional activator of miR172b with enrichment ~5-fold at binding site S1 located 1.2 kb upstream of miR172b transcription start.

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors

evidence.title: The Critical Role of miRNAs in Regulation of Flowering Time and Flower Development
evidence.authors: Waheed S, Zeng L
evidence.evidence_sentence: In Arabidopsis miR172 negatively regulate the expression of AP2 and five AP2-like genes including TARGET OF EAT 1 (TOE1), TOE2, TOE3, SCHNARCHZAPFEN (SNZ) and SCHLAFMUTZE (SMZ).

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors

evidence.title: Orchestration of the Floral Transition and Floral Development in Arabidopsis by the Bifunctional Transcription Factor APETALA2
evidence.authors: Yant L, Mathieu J, Dinh TT, Ott F, Lanz C, Wollmann H, Chen X, Schmid M

edges.mechanism: AP2 binds AP1 regulatory regions (ChIP-seq direct targets)

evidence.title: Orchestration of the Floral Transition and Floral Development in Arabidopsis by the Bifunctional Transcription Factor APETALA2
evidence.authors: Yant L, Mathieu J, Dinh TT, Ott F, Lanz C, Wollmann H, Chen X, Schmid M
evidence.evidence_sentence: Other Direct Targets: SEP3, AP1, TOE3, ARF3/ETTIN, AGL44

evidence.title: Florigen activation complex forms via multifaceted assembly in Arabidopsis

evidence.evidence_sentence: FT is not recruited by 14-3-3 alone, but that it interacts with the DNA-FD-14-3-3 complex through two interfaces, one of which binds DNA via the unstructured C terminus of FT

edges.mechanism: FT-FD complex activates AP1 at the shoot apical meristem
